behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser logic on one input string. MOCK-MISS: this behavior is about segmentation logic itself, not cross-module state handoff. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess required; a direct function call can reproduce the incident shape. Rejected higher levels: behavior text only asks that newline statements be segmented, with no requirement to execute shell or hooks end-to-end.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test the segmentation function with a multi-line shell snippet (`VAR=1\nOTHER=2\ngh pr create ...`) and assert it returns distinct statement segments with `gh pr create` isolated as its own segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: This is classifier correctness on a specific command pattern. MOCK-MISS: bug is trigger detection logic on parsed segments; can fail without module wiring. REAL-INFRA: no need to run real `gh` or shell. Rejected Integration/System: behavior text asks recognition of command text pattern, not process execution or file/state effects.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test classification with parsed segments representing assignments then `gh pr create`; assert decision is `pr_create_trigger` (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Negative classification case for adjacent syntax. Failure boundary is still command-pattern logic in one component. No real infra or cross-module state required. Rejected higher levels: behavior explicitly scopes to not misclassifying `gh issue create`, which is local parsing/classification, not hook orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test classifier input with assignments + `gh issue create`; assert result is not PR-create trigger.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: This crosses module boundaries: trigger result -> PR identifier reconstruction -> state persistence. Unit alone risks missing handoff/serialization mismatches. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed; temp local filesystem is sufficient. (b) no AI output dependency. (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejected System: behavior text says reconstruct and persist state, not validate real subprocess/HTTP behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test wiring detection output into state writer using a temp directory; verify persisted state contains the expected PR identifier fields consumable by downstream review loop logic.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is contract mismatch between producer hook and consumer hook via shared state format. This is classic multi-module handoff, so Integration is minimum. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real network/subprocess; no model output; no multi-agentic sequence. Rejected System: behavior text requires read/enforce compatibility, not real OS-level hook dispatch.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test runs post-tool state write path, then invokes pre-tool enforcement reader against that file in temp fs; assert enforcement activates for the matching PR context.
